evals.prompt: Our team just agreed to use PostgreSQL as the primary database for our new SaaS platform, choosing it over MongoDB and SQLite after weighing ACID requirements, our existing expertise, and the need for complex relational queries in reporting. The CTO has approved it; the decision is final. The repo has no architecture-decision docs yet (just src/ and package.json). Please record this decision properly so future engineers understand the reasoning and don't re-litigate it.
evals.expected_output: A first ADR created from scratch under docs/adrs/, numbered 0001, in MADR format with Accepted status and a runtime date, plus a README index.
expectations: Creates the ADR under docs/adrs/ (the default directory when none exists)
Names the file 0001-<kebab-title>.md — zero-padded to four digits, starting at 0001
The document starts with a '# 0001. <Title>' heading, with Status and Date as list items beneath it
Status is 'Accepted' and the Date is a real ISO (YYYY-MM-DD) date obtained at run time, not a guessed or placeholder value
Contains the four MADR sections in order: Context and drivers, Considered options, Decision, Consequences
The Considered options section lists at least two options (e.g. PostgreSQL, MongoDB, SQLite)
Creates a README.md index in the ADR directory with a table row linking 0001 to its file

evals.prompt: We've settled on an API versioning strategy: URL path versioning (/v1/, /v2/) with a minimum 6-month deprecation window for old versions. We also seriously considered header-based and query-parameter versioning. We already document architecture decisions — the existing records are in docs/adr/. Add this new decision following the conventions already in place, and update the index.
evals.expected_output: A new ADR 0003 added to the existing docs/adr/ directory in the established format, with the README index updated and prior rows preserved.
expectations: Places the new ADR in the existing docs/adr/ directory (not docs/adrs/ or any other path)
Numbers the new ADR 0003 (the highest existing is 0002) with a kebab-case 0003-<title>.md filename
Status is 'Accepted' and the Date is a real ISO date obtained at run time
The new ADR has the four MADR sections (Context and drivers, Considered options, Decision, Consequences) and lists at least two options
Appends a README row for 0003 linking its number to the file
Leaves the existing README rows for 0001 and 0002 intact (nothing deleted or overwritten)

evals.prompt: ADR 0002 in docs/adrs/ decided to store user sessions in memory. That no longer holds — we now run multiple replicas behind a load balancer, so in-memory sessions break horizontal scaling and drop on restart. We're moving session storage to Redis (we also considered sticky sessions and a database-backed session table; Redis won for speed, shared access, and built-in TTLs). Record the new decision and mark the old one as replaced, following the conventions in docs/adrs/.
evals.expected_output: A new ADR 0003 capturing the Redis decision and naming the record it replaces, ADR 0002 marked Superseded-by-0003, ADR 0001 untouched, and the index updated.
expectations: Creates a new docs/adrs/0003-<title>.md that is well-formed: a '# 0003. <Title>' heading, Status and Date list items, and the four MADR sections in order
The new ADR's Date is today's date obtained at run time, not a placeholder
The new ADR's body names ADR 0002 as the record it replaces
Changes ADR 0002's Status line to 'Superseded-by-0003' and otherwise leaves its sections and prose intact
Leaves ADR 0001 unchanged and still Accepted
Updates the README index: adds a row for 0003 and reflects that 0002 is superseded, preserving the existing rows

evals.prompt: ADR 0002 in docs/adrs/ was recorded as Proposed while we trialled feature flags for risky releases. The trial succeeded and we've formally adopted the approach. Update the record so it reflects that the decision is accepted, and update the index to match. This is a status change to an existing decision, not a new decision.
evals.expected_output: ADR 0002's status transitioned from Proposed to Accepted with the index updated, no new ADR created, and the other records untouched.
expectations: Changes ADR 0002's '- Status:' line from Proposed to Accepted
Updates the README index row for 0002 to show Accepted (no longer Proposed)
Creates no new ADR — this is a status change, not a new decision
Changes only the Status line of ADR 0002: its sections, prose, and original Date are preserved
Leaves ADR 0001 unchanged and still Accepted
